sections.paragraph:
ἠχέω fut. ἠχήσω LXX; aor. ἤχησα LXX (s. next entry; Hes. et al.) in our lit. only intr. (cp. 3 Km 1:41; Is 16:11; Mel., Fgm. 8b, 28) sound, ring out of brass instruments χαλκὸς ἠχῶν (cp. Hdt. 4, 200; Pla., Prot. 329a) 1 Cor 13:1. ἤχησεν σάλπιγξ Κυρίου GJs 8:3 (cp. PsSol 8:1 σάλπιγγος ἠχούσης σφαγὴν καὶ ὄλεθρον). Roar, thunder of the sea (cp. Himerius, Or. 40 [=Or. 6], 1 ἠχοῦσα θάλασσα; Ps 45:4; Jer 5:22) Lk 21:25 v.l. (on the Peshitta here, s. MBlack, An Aramaic Approach3, ’67, 261f).—DELG s.v. ἠχή.

fut. fut. = fut.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

aor. aor. = aorist

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

intr. intr. = intransitive

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

PsSol PsSol = Psalms of Solomon—List 2

Himerius Himerius , IV a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 441
